title: King Richard III
summaries: "King Richard III" by William Shakespeare is a play written around 1592–1594 that depicts the ruthless rise to power of Richard, Duke of Gloucester. Determined to seize the English throne despite his status as the king's youngest brother, Richard manipulates, deceives, and murders his way through the royal court. Through false promises, strategic marriages, and calculated assassinations—including his own family members—he eliminates every obstacle in his path. But as paranoia grows and his victims' ghosts haunt him, rebellion brews and nemesis approaches. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
